community.hrobot.boot.description: Set the boot configuration for a dedicated server.

server_number.description: The server number of the server whose boot configuration to adjust.

regular_boot.description: If this option is provided, all special boot configurations are removed and the installed operating system will be booted up next (assuming it is bootable). Precisely one of O(regular_boot), O(rescue), O(install_linux), O(install_vnc), O(install_windows), O(install_plesk), and O(install_cpanel) must be provided.

rescue.description: If this option is provided, the rescue system will be activated for the next boot. Precisely one of O(regular_boot), O(rescue), O(install_linux), O(install_vnc), O(install_windows), O(install_plesk), and O(install_cpanel) must be provided.

install_linux.description: If this option is provided, a Linux system install will be activated for the next boot. Precisely one of O(regular_boot), O(rescue), O(install_linux), O(install_vnc), O(install_windows), O(install_plesk), and O(install_cpanel) must be provided.

install_vnc.description: If this option is provided, a VNC installation will be activated for the next boot. Precisely one of O(regular_boot), O(rescue), O(install_linux), O(install_vnc), O(install_windows), O(install_plesk), and O(install_cpanel) must be provided.

install_windows.description: If this option is provided, a Windows installation will be activated for the next boot. Precisely one of O(regular_boot), O(rescue), O(install_linux), O(install_vnc), O(install_windows), O(install_plesk), and O(install_cpanel) must be provided.

install_plesk.description: If this option is provided, a Plesk installation will be activated for the next boot. Precisely one of O(regular_boot), O(rescue), O(install_linux), O(install_vnc), O(install_windows), O(install_plesk), and O(install_cpanel) must be provided.

install_cpanel.description: If this option is provided, a cPanel installation will be activated for the next boot. Precisely one of O(regular_boot), O(rescue), O(install_linux), O(install_vnc), O(install_windows), O(install_plesk), and O(install_cpanel) must be provided.

state.description: Defines whether the IP will be routed or not. If set to V(routed), O(value) must be specified.

value.description: The new value for the failover IP address. Required when setting O(state) to V(routed).

timeout.description: Timeout to use when routing or unrouting the failover IP. Note that the API call returns when the failover IP has been successfully routed to the new address, respectively successfully unrouted.

community.hrobot.failover_ip_info.short_description: Retrieve information on Hetzner's failover IPs
community.hrobot.failover_ip_info.description: Retrieve information on Hetzner's failover IPs.

community.hrobot.firewall.description: Manage Hetzner's dedicated server firewall. Note that idempotency check for TCP flags simply compares strings and does not try to interpret the rules. This might change in the future.

server_ip.description: The server's main IP address. Exactly one of O(server_ip) and O(server_number) must be specified. Note that Hetzner deprecated identifying the server's firewall by the server's main IP. Using this option can thus stop working at any time in the future. Use O(server_number) instead.

server_number.description: The server's number. Exactly one of O(server_ip) and O(server_number) must be specified.

filter_ipv6.description: Whether to filter IPv6 traffic as well. IPv4 traffic is always filtered, IPv6 traffic filtering needs to be explicitly enabled.

state.description: Status of the firewall. Firewall is active if state is V(present), and disabled if state is V(absent).

update_timeout.description: Timeout to use when configuring the firewall. Note that the API call returns before the firewall has been successfully set up.

wait_for_configured.description: Whether to wait until the firewall has been successfully configured before determining what to do, and before returning from the module. The API returns status C(in progress) when the firewall is currently being configured. If this happens, the module will try again until the status changes to C(active) or C(disabled). Please note that there is a request limit. If you have to do multiple updates, it can be better to disable waiting, and regularly use M(community.hrobot.firewall_info) to query status.

wait_delay.description: Delay to wait (in seconds) before checking again whether the firewall has been configured.

timeout.description: Timeout (in seconds) for waiting for firewall to be configured.

server_ip.description: The server's main IP address. Exactly one of O(server_ip) and O(server_number) must be specified. Note that Hetzner deprecated identifying the server's firewall by the server's main IP. Using this option can thus stop working at any time in the future. Use O(server_number) instead.

server_number.description: The server's number. Exactly one of O(server_ip) and O(server_number) must be specified.

wait_for_configured.description: Whether to wait until the firewall has been successfully configured before returning from the module. The API returns status C(in progress) when the firewall is currently being configured. If this happens, the module will try again until the status changes to C(active) or C(disabled). Please note that there is a request limit. If you have to do multiple updates, it can be better to disable waiting, and regularly use M(community.hrobot.firewall_info) to query status.

wait_delay.description: Delay to wait (in seconds) before checking again whether the firewall has been configured.

timeout.description: Timeout (in seconds) for waiting for firewall to be configured.

community.hrobot.reset.description: Reset a dedicated server with a software or hardware reset, or by requesting a manual reset.

server_number.description: The server number of the server to reset.

reset_type.description: How to reset the server. V(software) is a software reset. This should be similar to pressing Ctrl+Alt+Del on the keyboard. V(power) is a hardware reset similar to pressing the Power button. An ACPI signal is sent, and if the server is configured correctly, this will trigger a regular shutdown. V(hardware) is a hardware reset similar to pressing the Restart button. The power is cycled for the server. V(manual) is a manual reset. This requests a technician to manually do the shutdown while looking at the screen output. B(Be careful) and only use this when really necessary! Note that not every server supports every reset method! You can query the supported reset methods by using the RV(community.hrobot.reset_info#module:reset.type) return value of the M(community.hrobot.reset_info) module.

community.hrobot.reset_info.short_description: Query information on the resetter of a dedicated server
community.hrobot.reset_info.description: Query information on the resetter of a dedicated server.

server_number.description: The server number of the server to query its resetter.

community.hrobot.reverse_dns.short_description: Set or remove reverse DNS entry for IP
community.hrobot.reverse_dns.description: Allows to set, update or remove a reverse DNS entry for an IP address.

ip.description: The IP address to set or remove a reverse DNS entry for.

state.description: Whether to set or update (V(present)) or delete (V(absent)) the reverse DNS entry for O(ip).

value.description: The reverse DNS entry for O(ip). Required if O(state=present).

community.hrobot.server.description: Allows to update server information. Right now the API only supports updating the server's name.

server_number.description: The server number of the server to update.

server_name.description: The server's name. If this option is not provided, it will not be adjusted.

community.hrobot.server_info.short_description: Query information on one or more servers
community.hrobot.server_info.description: Query information on one or more servers.

server_number.description: Limit result list to server with this number.

server_name.description: Limit result list to servers of this name.

full_info.description: Whether to provide full information for every server. Setting this to V(true) requires one REST call per server, which is slow and reduces your rate limit. Use with care. When O(server_number) is specified, this option is always treated as having value V(true).

community.hrobot.ssh_key.short_description: Add, remove or update SSH key
community.hrobot.ssh_key.description: Add, remove or update an SSH key stored in Hetzner's Robot.

state.description: Whether to make sure a public SSH key is present or absent. V(present) makes sure that the SSH key is available, and potentially updates names for existing SSH public keys. V(absent) makes sure that the SSH key is not available. The fingerprint or public key data is used for matching the key.

name.description: The public key's name. Required if O(state=present), and ignored if O(state=absent).

fingerprint.description: The MD5 fingerprint of the public SSH key to remove. One of O(public_key) and O(fingerprint) are required if O(state=absent).

public_key.description: The public key data in OpenSSH format. Example: V(ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1yc+..). One of O(public_key) and O(fingerprint) are required if O(state=absent). Required if O(state=present).

community.hrobot.ssh_key_info.description: List information on all your SSH keys stored in Hetzner's Robot.

community.hrobot.storagebox.short_description: Modify a storage box's basic configuration
community.hrobot.storagebox.description: Modify a storage box's basic configuration.

id.description: The ID of the storage box to modify.

name.description: The name of the storage box.

samba.description: Whether the storage box is accessible through SAMBA.

webdav.description: Whether the storage box is accessible through WebDAV.

ssh.description: Whether the storage box is accessible through SSH.

external_reachability.description: Whether the storage box is externally reachable.

zfs.description: Whether the ZFS directory is visible.

community.hrobot.storagebox_info.short_description: Query information on one or more storage boxes
community.hrobot.storagebox_info.description: Query information on one or more storage box.

storagebox_id.description: Limit result list to storage boxes with this ID.

linked_server_number.description: Limit result list to storage boxes linked to the server with this number. Ignored when O(storagebox_id) has been specified, or when O(hetzner_token) has been specified.

full_info.description: Whether to provide full information for every storage box. Setting this to V(true) requires one REST call per storage box, which is slow and reduces your rate limit. Use with care. When O(storagebox_id) is specified, this option is always treated as having value V(true). B(Note) that this option has no effect if O(hetzner_token) is specified.

community.hrobot.storagebox_set_password.short_description: (Re)set the password for a storage box
community.hrobot.storagebox_set_password.description: (Re)set the password for a storage box.

id.description: The ID of the storage box to modify.

password.description: The new password for the storage box. If not provided, a random password will be created by the Robot API and returned as RV(password). This option is required if O(hetzner_token) is provided, since the new API does not support setting (and returning) a random password.

community.hrobot.storagebox_snapshot.short_description: Create, update, or delete a snapshot of a storage box
community.hrobot.storagebox_snapshot.description: Create, update comment, or delete a snapshot of a storage box.

storagebox_id.description: The ID of the storage box to snapshot.

snapshot_name.description: The name of the snapshot to comment or delete. The snapshot name is automatically generated and should not be specified when creating a snapshot. Required when setting O(state) to V(absent), or when O(snapshot_comment) is specified.

snapshot_comment.description: The comment to set for the snapshot.

community.hrobot.storagebox_snapshot_inf.short_description: Query the snapshots for a storage box
community.hrobot.storagebox_snapshot_inf.description: Query the snapshots for a storage box.

storagebox_id.description: The ID of the storage box to query.

community.hrobot.storagebox_snapshot_pla.short_description: Modify a storage box's snapshot plans
community.hrobot.storagebox_snapshot_pla.description: Enable, modify, and disable the snapshot plans of a storage box.

storagebox_id.description: The ID of the storage box to modify.

plans.description: The storage plan configurations. Note that right now there must be exactly one element. All date and time parameters are in UTC.

community.hrobot.storagebox_snapshot_pla.short_description: Query the snapshot plans for a storage box
community.hrobot.storagebox_snapshot_pla.description: Query the snapshot plans for a storage box.

storagebox_id.description: The ID of the storage box to query.

community.hrobot.storagebox_subaccount.short_description: Create, update, or delete a subaccount for a storage box
community.hrobot.storagebox_subaccount.description: Create, update, or delete a subaccount for a storage box.

storagebox_id.description: The ID of the storage box to query.

password_mode.description: Controls how password updates are handled. If C(update-if-provided), the password always updated if provided (default). If C(ignore-if-exists), password is only used during creation. If C(set-to-random), password is reset to a randomly generated one. Note that this is not supported by the new API (when O(hetzner_token) is set). The value has been deprecated in community.hrobot 2.7.0 and will be removed from community.hrobot 3.0.0. When a new subaccount is created, the password is set to the specified one if O(password) is provided, and a random password is set if O(password) is not provided.

username.description: Username of the subaccount. Required when using O(idempotence=username) for updates or deletion of a subaccount. If O(idempotence=username) and this is not specified, a new subaccount will always be created. If O(idempotence=comment), this option is ignored, as the Hetzner API does not allow to chose or modify the username.

password.description: Password to use or change. See O(password_mode) for how and when this is used. Will be ignored if O(password_mode=set-to-random).

homedirectory.description: Home directory of the subaccount. Required only when creating a subaccount (O(state=present)).

external_reachability.description: Enable or disable external reachability (from outside Hetzner's networks).

comment.description: A custom comment for the subaccount. Is required when using O(idempotence=comment) for updates or deletion of a subaccount.

idempotence.description: Select which attribute to use to check subaccount existence. If set to C(username), then subaccounts are identified by their username. Note that usernames cannot be specified on creation, so you need to use different module arguments for creation and updating. If set to C(comment), then subaccounts are identified by their comment. If there already exist more than one subaccount with the given comment, the module will fail.

community.hrobot.storagebox_subaccount_i.short_description: Query the subaccounts for a storage box
community.hrobot.storagebox_subaccount_i.description: Query the subaccounts for a storage box.

storagebox_id.description: The ID of the storage box to query.

vlan.description: The vSwitch's VLAN ID. Range can be from 4000 to 4091. In order to identify a vSwitch both name and VLAN must match. If not, a new vSwitch will be created.

name.description: The vSwitch's name. In order to identify a vSwitch both name and VLAN must match. If not, a new vSwitch will be created.

state.description: State of the vSwitch. VSwitch is created if state is V(present), and deleted if state is V(absent). V(absent) just cancels the vSwitch at the end of the current day. When cancelling, you have to specify O(servers=[]) if you want to actively remove the servers in the vSwitch.

servers.description: List of server identifiers (server's numeric ID or server's main IPv4 or IPv6). If servers is not specified, servers are not going to be deleted.

wait.description: Whether to wait until the vSwitch has been successfully configured before determining what to do, and before returning from the module. The API returns status C(in process) when the vSwitch is currently being set up in the servers. If this happens, the module will try again until the status changes to C(ready) or server has been removed from vSwitch. Please note that if you disable wait while deleting and removing servers module will fail with C(VSWITCH_IN_PROCESS) error.

wait_delay.description: Delay to wait (in seconds) before checking again whether the vSwitch servers has been configured.

timeout.description: Timeout (in seconds) for waiting for vSwitch servers to be configured.
